preamble: Monophthongization occurs when a diphthong like 'ie' simplifies back into a single vowel sound, often occurring in specific dialects or as a further evolution of a diphthongized sound.

examples.note: Dialectal simplification of Spanish 'ie' back into monophthong [e].

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
